agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"order_state_machine","to":"downstream_consumers","status":"risk","issue":"No event log or outbox pattern means downstream services have no way to know about stuck orders. If a transition fails silently after partial side effects, there is no mechanism to detect or reconcile the inconsistency."},{"from":"order_state_machine","to":"central_log_aggregator","status":"risk","issue":"30-second ingestion delay means the on-call engineer is always investigating a stale picture. During a fast-moving incident with cascading failures, they are making decisions on data that is 30-90 seconds old."},{"from":"alerting_pipeline","to":"pagerduty","status":"risk","issue":"Alert threshold of >5 failures/min is a single static threshold. No rate-of-change detection, no anomaly detection. A slow degradation (4 failures/min sustained for hours) will never page anyone, silently accumulating stuck orders."}],"contract_compliance":"No contract enforcement visible. State transitions have no schema validation or idempotency guarantees documented. If a caller retries a transition that partially succeeded, behavior is undefined.","data_flow_integrity":"CRITICAL GAP: No event log or outbox means failed transitions are fire-and-forget. There is no audit trail beyond logs, and logs are not a reliable source of truth for state reconstruction. If the log pipeline drops events, the failure is invisible."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"gaps","gaps":["No correlation IDs across services — when the on-call engineer sees a failed transition, they cannot trace it upstream to the API call or downstream to the effect","Structured fields are good (request_id, order_id, from/to state, actor_id, duration_ms, error_code) but missing: retry_count, idempotency_key, upstream_caller, payload_hash","No log for the EXPECTED state vs ACTUAL state when a transition is rejected — critical for diagnosing 'stuck in PROCESSING' scenarios where the order thinks it is in a different state than the caller expects","No dedicated log event for 'order has been in state X for longer than Y duration' — stuck orders are only detectable by querying, not by observing"]},"metrics":{"coverage":"minimal — two metrics is not enough for production diagnosis","gaps":["No gauge for orders_in_state{state=PROCESSING} — the most basic metric for detecting stuck orders is missing. The on-call engineer must query the database to answer 'how many orders are stuck right now?'","No histogram of time-in-state — cannot detect orders aging in PROCESSING without a custom query","No metric for transition attempts vs successes (retry visibility)","No metric segmented by actor_id or order_type — cannot isolate if failures are scoped to one integration partner or one order category","No queue depth or backpressure metric if transitions are processed asynchronously"]},"tracing":{"coverage":"absent","gaps":["No distributed tracing at all. This is the single largest incident response gap. When orders are stuck in PROCESSING, the engineer cannot answer: Was the transition attempted? Did it timeout waiting on a downstream call? Did a dependent service fail? They are left grep-ing logs by order_id across multiple systems manually.","No span correlation means no flame graph, no waterfall, no latency breakdown per dependency"]},"debugging_capability":"POOR. The on-call engineer at 3am can answer 'are transitions failing?' (yes, the alert told them). They CANNOT efficiently answer: Why are they failing? Which orders are affected? What downstream impact exists? Is it getting worse or recovering? They will spend the first 15-20 minutes of the incident just building situational awareness by manually querying logs and the database."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"Database lock contention causes transition timeouts — orders stuck in PROCESSING","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Add a transition timeout with automatic rollback or dead-letter mechanism. An order stuck in PROCESSING for >N minutes should emit a metric AND be automatically flagged for reprocessing."},{"scenario":"Partial failure: transition side-effects fire but state update fails — order in inconsistent state","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Implement outbox pattern or saga with compensation. Without this, every partial failure requires manual database intervention at 3am."},{"scenario":"Downstream service outage causes all transitions from PROCESSING to SHIPPED to fail","handling":"partial","recommendation":"The alert will fire, but the engineer cannot distinguish 'our service is broken' from 'a dependency is broken' without tracing. Add dependency health metrics and circuit breaker with fallback state (e.g., PROCESSING_AWAITING_RETRY)."},{"scenario":"Slow degradation: 4 failures/min for 6 hours accumulates 1,440 stuck orders without ever paging","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Add a second alert on orders_in_state{state=PROCESSING} duration or count exceeding threshold. The rate alert alone is insufficient."},{"scenario":"Poison pill order: one malformed order causes repeated transition failures, consuming error budget","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Add per-order retry limit with dead-letter/quarantine state. Log the order_id prominently so the engineer can isolate it immediately."},{"scenario":"Clock skew or race condition: two concurrent transition attempts for same order","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Need optimistic locking or compare-and-swap on state transitions. Without idempotency guarantees, concurrent retries can corrupt state."}],"retry_config":"Not documented. Unknown if retries exist, and if they do, whether they use exponential backoff. This is a critical unknown — retries without backoff during an incident will amplify load and make recovery harder.","timeout_config":"Not documented. If transitions call downstream services, the timeout configuration determines whether stuck-in-PROCESSING is caused by hung outbound calls. The on-call engineer needs this information immediately.","circuit_breakers":"None mentioned. A downstream outage will cause unbounded failure accumulation rather than fast-failing with a clear error.","graceful_degradation":"None. There is no fallback behavior, no read-only mode, no ability to pause transitions and drain gracefully. The system either works or it doesn't."},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":false,"rollback_capable":false,"rollback_plan":"Unknown. No event log means if a bad deployment causes incorrect state transitions, there is no way to identify and revert affected orders without manual database forensics.","monitoring_coverage":"INSUFFICIENT. Can detect 'transitions are failing' but cannot diagnose why, cannot measure blast radius, cannot track recovery. The 3am engineer will be flying blind after the first 30 seconds of the incident.","on_call_documentation":"Runbook is 18 months stale. In a system with state machines, 18 months means the runbook likely references states, thresholds, or remediation steps that no longer exist. A stale runbook is worse than no runbook — it gives false confidence and wastes time.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":[],"new_services":["Distributed tracing backend (Jaeger/Tempo) — or at minimum, propagate a trace_id through all transition-related calls","Dead letter queue or reprocessing mechanism for failed transitions"],"new_queues_topics":["Failed transition dead-letter queue for orders that exceed retry limits","Optionally: state transition event stream (outbox) for auditability and replay"],"config_changes":["Add orders_in_state gauge metric","Add time_in_state histogram metric","Add alert for orders stuck in PROCESSING > threshold duration","Add alert for slow-burn failure accumulation (count-based, not just rate-based)","Configure circuit breakers for downstream dependencies"],"infra_provisioning":["Event store or outbox table for transition auditability and replay capability"]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":false,"reversible":false,"data_preserving":false,"large_table_impact":false,"details":"Cannot assess — no migration information provided. However, adding an outbox table or dead-letter mechanism will require schema changes to the orders database."},"verdict":"NOT READY"},"escalations":[{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"No distributed tracing across service boundaries","reason":"When orders are stuck in PROCESSING, the on-call engineer cannot determine if the root cause is in this service or a dependency. They will waste 15+ minutes of incident time manually correlating logs across systems. At 3am under pressure, this is the difference between a 20-minute incident and a 2-hour incident.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"No replay or reprocessing capability for failed state transitions","reason":"Every stuck order requires manual database intervention to recover. At scale (hundreds of stuck orders), this turns a P2 incident into a P1 because recovery time scales linearly with the number of affected orders. An outbox or event log is required for operational recovery.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"No gauge metric for orders currently in each state","reason":"The most fundamental question during this incident — 'how many orders are stuck?' — cannot be answered from metrics. The engineer must query production database directly, which adds latency, requires DB access (which on-call may not have), and adds load to an already stressed system.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"Stale runbook and no circuit breakers create compounding incident risk","reason":"The 18-month-old runbook will mislead the on-call engineer, and the lack of circuit breakers means a dependency failure will cascade into unbounded stuck orders. Together, these mean the incident will escalate before it stabilizes.","routed_to":"Coach K"}],"confidence":{"level":85,"high_confidence_areas":["The observability gaps are clear and well-understood — no tracing, insufficient metrics, stale runbook","The lack of replay/reprocessing is a hard operational gap with no workaround","The alert threshold gap (slow burn below 5/min) is a concrete missed failure mode"],"low_confidence_areas":["Retry and timeout configuration is undocumented — the actual behavior may be better or worse than assumed","Database access patterns and locking behavior are unknown — the contention failure mode is inferred","Downstream dependency topology is unknown — the blast radius assessment is based on the general architecture pattern"],"assumptions":["The service calls at least one downstream dependency during state transitions","There is no separate reconciliation job that periodically detects and fixes stuck orders","The on-call engineer does not have pre-built database queries for diagnosing stuck orders","The 30-second log ingestion delay is the best case, not worst case"]}}
